title: n8n Webhook Basics: Triggering Workflows from External Events
description: Learn how to set up webhook triggers in n8n so external apps, APIs, and services can automatically start your automation workflows.

resources.title: n8n Webhook Node Documentation
resources.description: Official docs on setting up and configuring webhook triggers in n8n

resources.title: n8n Getting Started Guide
resources.description: Introduction to n8n workflows, nodes, and the automation interface

resources.title: Create a webhook-triggered workflow
resources.description: Set up a webhook in n8n and test it by sending a POST request with curl

quiz.question: What is the primary purpose of setting up a webhook in n8n?
options: To manually trigger workflows from the n8n interface
To allow external apps and services to automatically trigger workflows
To create new nodes within an existing workflow
quiz.explanation: Webhooks are designed to enable external applications to trigger workflows automatically, streamlining the automation process.

quiz.question: Which HTTP method is commonly used to test a webhook in n8n?
quiz.explanation: The POST method is typically used to send data to a webhook, allowing you to test its functionality in n8n.

quiz.question: What is the role of nodes in n8n workflows?
options: To serve as triggers for external applications
To represent individual steps that perform specific tasks
To store data permanently within n8n
To connect to the internet for external API calls
quiz.explanation: Nodes in n8n represent individual operations or tasks that can be executed as part of a workflow.

practicePrompts.label: Set Up Webhook
practicePrompts.prompt: Guide me through the steps to set up a webhook trigger in n8n for my automation workflow.
practicePrompts.followUp: Try testing the webhook by sending a POST request using curl.

practicePrompts.label: Test Webhook with Curl
practicePrompts.instruction: Send a test request to your webhook.
practicePrompts.prompt: Show me how to use curl to send a POST request to the webhook I just created in n8n.
practicePrompts.followUp: Modify the POST request to include JSON data and see how n8n handles it.

practicePrompts.label: Explore Webhook Responses
practicePrompts.instruction: Check the response from your webhook.
practicePrompts.prompt: Explain how to view the response from the webhook after sending a POST request in n8n.
practicePrompts.followUp: Experiment by changing the response format in n8n and observe the differences.
